findings.title: Commit messages follow conventional commits format

commit_messages.message: test: add tests for malformed configuration file handling
test: add tests for API endpoints and metadata extraction functions
test: add tests for dependency extraction functions
test: add tests for codebase scanning and file parsing functions
docs: document duplicate imports and return type fix bug track
feat: enhance codebase scanning, metadata extraction, and file parsing
feat: add scripts and entry_points extraction from pyproject.toml
test: add tests for pyproject.toml scripts, entry_points, and error handling
docs: document duplicate imports and return type fix
fix: remove unused imports and fix duplicate import

findings.why_it_matters: Conventional commits enable automated changelog generation and improve project maintainability
findings.notes: All commits follow the recommended format from CLAUDE.md

findings.title: Commit messages do not start with uppercase letters

commit_messages_lowercased.original: test: add tests for malformed configuration file handling
test: add tests for API endpoints and metadata extraction functions
test: add tests for dependency extraction functions
test: add tests for codebase scanning and file parsing functions
docs: document duplicate imports and return type fix bug track
feat: enhance codebase scanning, metadata extraction, and file parsing
feat: add scripts and entry_points extraction from pyproject.toml
test: add tests for pyproject.toml scripts, entry_points, and error handling
docs: document duplicate imports and return type fix
fix: remove unused imports and fix duplicate import

findings.why_it_matters: CLAUDE.md explicitly states not to use uppercase letters in commit messages
findings.notes: All commits comply with CLAUDE.md commit message rules

findings.title: Type hints added to main.py function signatures

changes.after: def main(model: Optional[str], show_help: bool) -> None:
changes.improvement: Added type hints for all parameters and return type

changes.before: def analyze(path, output, format, verbose, use_agents):
changes.after: def analyze(path: str, output: Optional[str] = None, format: str = 'text', verbose: bool = False, use_agents: bool = False) -> int:
changes.improvement: Added type hints for all parameters and return type

changes.after: def diagram(path: str, output: Optional[str] = None, verbose: bool = False, use_agents: bool = False) -> int:
changes.improvement: Added type hints for all parameters and return type

changes.after: def api(path: str, output: Optional[str] = None, verbose: bool = False, use_agents: bool = False) -> int:
changes.improvement: Added type hints for all parameters and return type

changes.after: def setup(path: str, output: Optional[str] = None) -> int:
changes.improvement: Added type hints for all parameters and return type

findings.why_it_matters: Type hints improve code documentation and enable static analysis
